review: Excellent service. It's overall a really beautifully designed restaurant. Whether you're sitting close to the Kitchen, dining room, or in the garden, it's all very intimate. The fresh baked pita is some of the best that I have had in the city, and the hummus was bangin'. We ended up going for the "A Taste of Suraya" which was $58 a person: 6 mezzas to share, a main meat or vegetable, and dessert. Although the food here is pricey, it was a very memorable experience similar to that of Zahav.
